evidence: We seek to deploy our capital resources efficiently in technology to support operations including... machine learning algorithms, improving our process efficiency... and supporting evolving technological advancements such as artificial intelligence.
technology transformation efforts, including the adoption of evolving technological advancements such as artificial intelligence

Technology expenses decreased by $24.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2025... primarily due to a reduction in staff-related expenses of $19.0 million and a $3.2 million reduction in third-party expenses

machine learning algorithms, improving our process efficiency, modernizing and expanding our systems, and supporting and expanding our logistics infrastructure, and supporting evolving technological advancements such as artificial intelligence
Our competitors may outpace us in incorporating AI into their product offerings, engagement with customers, and to create efficiencies

a shift in user search behavior has started... an increasing number of individuals transitioning from traditional search engines like Google to AI platform answer engines such as ChatGPT, Grok, and Copilot

any integration of artificial intelligence in our or any service providers' or business partners' operations, products or services is expected to pose new or unknown cybersecurity risks and challenges

content, analyses, or recommendations generated by AI programs, if deficient, inaccurate, or biased, could adversely impact our business, financial condition, and operational results, as well as our reputation

a shift in user search behavior... transitioning from traditional search engines like Google to AI platform answer engines such as ChatGPT, Grok, and Copilot... could have a material adverse effect on our business

capex_mentions.quote: Technology expenses $90,276 [thousand] for year ended December 31, 2025

limitations: AI investment intensity: technology expense figures ($90M) cover all technology broadly (cloud, web, logistics, AI); no AI-specific spend is isolated, so score capped at 3 rather than 4.

AI narrative concreteness: named use cases (ML algorithms, process efficiency, product search) are described at initiative level but no deployment metrics or quantified outcomes are disclosed; score capped at 3.
Technology spend is declining year-over-year; AI adoption is framed partly as a cost-reduction driver rather than a growth investment.
The filing date of 2026-02-24 is noted; this appears to be a fiscal year 2025 10-K for the reconstituted Bed Bath & Beyond e-commerce entity.
